body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

turn-end-terminal-text pins the terminal-text end signal: the assistant produces a final plain-text message that closes the turn with no tool use, and the classifier drives working→ready off the adapter-specific end token. The User-observable signal is the `ready → working → ready` arc reaching `ready` regardless of which adapter-specific signal closes the turn. Pi supports this natively — the pi parser maps `stopReason=stop` on the final assistant event to `turn_done`, settling working→ready (this is the real-transcript behavior confirmed in the pi parser tests and #119). A one-line plain-text prompt (no tool) produces exactly this terminal-text close.

irrlicht observes the full arc: pi writes one JSONL rollout file per session under `~/.pi/agent/sessions/<cwd-slug>/<ts>_<UUID>.jsonl` on launch (FilesUnderRoot, non-lazy), so the daemon sees the rollout session born ready, the working flip on the first transcript activity, and the working→ready settle when the terminal-text turn completes, plus pid_discovered and one process_exited at REPL teardown.

UNLIKE the headless `pi --print` shape (basic-turn / session-end), this cell is recorded with the INTERACTIVE driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh): a single `send` of a plain-text prompt + `wait_turn` keeps pi alive long enough for the daemon to observe the working→ready settle as a distinct arc before teardown. Headless `pi --print` exits too fast and the terminal working→ready settle races process_exited (concern C6), so script-mode is the right shape for an end-signal cell. The cell overlaps basic-turn intentionally but isolates the end-of-turn mechanism (terminal stop_reason allow-list), not the broader happy-path lifecycle.
caveats: Pi cannot accept a pre-assigned UUID — it generates one itself. The interactive driver discovers the UUID from the session header on line 1 of the freshly-created rollout transcript.

The live REPL may briefly emit a proc-<PID> pre-session born ready before the UUID-keyed rollout transcript appears; the spec anchors the turn arc to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher binds the UUID rollout (the pre-session never enters working), then chains later phases via same_session_as.

Script-mode (send + wait_turn) is used instead of headless `pi --print` so the daemon observes the working→ready settle as a distinct arc; headless exits too fast and the terminal settle races process_exited (concern C6).

sources.note: Identity.Name=pi, Source variant FilesUnderRoot under ~/.pi/agent/sessions/, ExactName{Name: "pi"} process matcher.
stopReason=stop on the final assistant event maps to turn_done (working→ready) — the terminal-text end signal (#119).

Interactive tmux REPL driver supporting send/slash/wait_turn/interrupt/sleep; keeps pi alive across the single plain-text turn so the working→ready settle is observed, then kills the tmux session once → single process_exited.
